title: eriogonum spergulinum var. reddingianum
intro: Eriogonum spergulinum var. reddingianum, also known as Redding Buckwheat, is a variety of the Eriogonum spergulinum plant, a flowering plant native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant's roots and leaves have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Redding Buckwheat include reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting immune function. Eriogonum spergulinum var. reddingianum can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve, and is also used as a food source, particularly in baking and as a nutritious addition to salads.
